footer.description: EMUS - Electric Motorcycle of Universite de Sherbrooke
footer.note: Building for MotoStudent 2027 - MotorLand Aragon, Spain

hero.title: From Sherbrooke to MotorLand Aragon
hero.lead: EMUS (Electric Motorcycle of Universite de Sherbrooke) is a student engineering team developing a 100% electric race motorcycle for the MotoStudent competition in Spain.

about.title: Design. Build. Race.
about.paragraph_1: MotoStudent is an international engineering competition where university teams design and build a race motorcycle prototype, evaluated at MotorLand Aragon (Alcaniz, Spain). Categories include Petrol and Electric.
about.paragraph_2: EMUS is competing in the Electric category, bringing together students from the Universite de Sherbrooke to engineer a fully electric race bike for MotoStudent 2027.
about.timeline_1_note: Now - System architecture, validation, supplier alignment

sponsors.title: Support the build
sponsors.paragraph: Support EMUS: funding, manufacturing, materials, and technical mentorship. Strategic partners help accelerate testing, manufacturing, and race readiness.

contact.title: Let's talk
contact.paragraph: Want to partner, mentor, or learn more about the project? Reach out and we'll connect you with the right team lead.
contact.based_in_note: Universite de Sherbrooke - Faculty of Engineering

hero.title: Engineering students building electric performance
hero.lead: EMUS is an engineering student team from Universite de Sherbrooke building a fully electric race bike for MotoStudent 2027.
hero.image_alt: EMUS team with the electric prototype on track

overview.title: Students building a competitive electric prototype
overview.paragraph_1: Multidisciplinary students in mechanical, electrical, controls, software, and business collaborate to design, build, and test a fully electric race bike for MotoStudent 2027.
overview.paragraph_2: We mix classroom theory with track-ready engineering: from chassis and battery design to testing and race operations.

photo.title: Team photo
photo.image_alt: EMUS team with the electric prototype on track

hero.title: Building a 100% electric race prototype
hero.lead: The project delivers a 100% electric prototype capable of competing at MotorLand Aragon in October 2027.

objectives.title: Race-ready for October 2027
objectives.paragraph_1: We are engineering a competitive electric motorcycle that meets MotoStudent technical requirements and performs reliably at MotorLand Aragon.
objectives.paragraph_2: Timeline highlights: design validation now; build and testing through 2027; final event Oct 2027.

focus.title: Systems in development
focus.card_1_body: Our goal this year is to improve motor and inverter design to capture the final performance gains. This includes tuning inverter control algorithms to push motor efficiency and power output close to thermal limits.
focus.card_2_body: Last year we designed a battery pack using high-performance cell technology. For 2027 we aim to update to next-generation cells and improve tuning of our custom active balancing BMS.
focus.card_3_body: For 2027, our focus is redesigning chassis architecture to optimize stiffness and reduce weight. In parallel, we are developing structural carbon fiber components to maximize reliability under racing loads.
focus.card_4_body: For 2027 we are designing our own ECU to control all vehicle systems and enable precise performance tuning.
focus.card_5_body: The 2025 bike introduced new sensors and telemetry systems for data-driven development. For 2027 we plan to add sensors and integrate their data deeper into vehicle control.
focus.card_6_body: The cooling system is receiving a major upgrade, including exploration of new methods to improve thermal performance.
focus.card_7_body: The 2027 fairings are being redesigned from the ground up and optimized using CFD plus wind tunnel testing.

timeline.title: Key milestones
timeline.item_1_note: In progress. System architecture, analysis, supplier alignment

hero.title: Support the EMUS electric build
hero.lead: Support EMUS: funding, manufacturing, materials, and technical mentorship.

offer.title: Official partner offer — Groupe Abbatiello
offer.body: Support EMUS while enjoying a meal! Use the EMUS15 promo code for 5% off at Pizza Salvatore, Jack le Coq, and Topla.

ways.title: Ways to support
ways.card_1_body: Accelerate manufacturing, testing, and race logistics.
ways.card_2_body: Machine time, composites, welding, and specialized processes.
ways.card_3_body: Metals, composites, fasteners, wiring, and testing equipment.
ways.card_4_body: Guidance on design, validation, reliability, and race preparation.
ways.card_5_body: Simulation licenses, track access, and test instrumentation.
ways.card_6_body: Lab access, benches, or storage to keep the build moving.

visibility.title: Showcase opportunities
visibility.body: Logos and activations will be placed on the bike, team apparel, pit setup, and communications. Placeholder assets are shown until sponsor logos are confirmed.

cta.title: Ready to partner?
cta.body: We'll tailor a package around visibility, recruitment, and technical collaboration.
cta.next_steps_1: Share your focus: funding, materials, or mentorship
cta.next_steps_2: We respond with needs and timelines
